untranslatable_reason: Signal requires options-specific inputs (theoretical option value, market price, bid-ask spread, volatility forecast confidence, mispricing margin %) that are not available in price/technical indicators. The core logic—comparing forward volatility forecasts to realized volatility—cannot be fully translated without explicit option pricing data and mispricing metrics. Used hvol_20 as a proxy for volatility regime, but this does not capture the options valuation mechanics central to the signal.
translation_notes: This is an options pricing signal from Natenberg's work. The key missing elements are: (1) theoretical option value (requires Black-Scholes or similar with input volatility), (2) market option price, (3) mispricing margin calculation, (4) liquidity filter (bid-ask spread), and (5) volatility forecast confidence assessment. A proper backtest would require an options module with pricing functions and options-specific data streams. The translated version uses hvol_20 (20-period historical volatility) as a weak proxy for volatility regime detection, but cannot express the option mispricing comparison logic.
